name: makerchip
description: VS Code extension for Makerchip IDE integration

makerchip.autoUpdateResources.description: Automatically update Makerchip reference data on activation

enumDescriptions: Ask whether to add reference data folder to workspace
Always add reference data folder to workspace
Never add reference data folder to workspace
makerchip.addResourcesFolder.description: Add Makerchip reference data folder to workspace (for LLMs)

makerchip.serverUrl.description: Base URL of the Makerchip server (for testing with local or staging servers)

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_compile
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compile and open TL-Verilog in Makerchip IDE.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens the Makerchip IDE in VS Code and compiles the current or specified TL-Verilog file, showing the compiled circuit diagram, waveforms, and Visual Debug output. IMPORTANT: Use this tool whenever the user wants to SEE, SHOW, DEMONSTRATE, VISUALIZE, or VIEW TL-Verilog code or examples - don't just show code in the chat. Also use when they want to run, compile, open, launch, or test Makerchip or TL-Verilog. When providing TL-Verilog examples, prefer opening them in Makerchip rather than only displaying the code. The visual IDE is the primary way to interact with TL-Verilog.

filePath.description: Optional path to a TL-Verilog file to open and compile. If not provided, uses the currently active editor.

code.description: Optional TL-Verilog code to compile. When provided, creates a new unsaved file with this code and opens it in Makerchip. Use this to show examples or demonstrate TL-Verilog features.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel. Use this to open multiple Makerchip panels for different designs or comparisons.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_ide_call
languageModelTools.userDescription: Call any Makerchip IDE method directly.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Invokes any method on the Makerchip IDE instance. Available methods include: compile(code), setCode(code, readOnly), getCode(lastChangeGeneration), setCodeFromURL(url, readOnly), activatePane(name), openPane(name, background). Use this for advanced IDE control like changing panes, setting code without compiling, or loading code from URLs. Methods like onReady, onCodeChange, onCompilationLog are callbacks and should not be called directly.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the method. Order matters.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_viz_image
languageModelTools.userDescription: Capture the current VIZ visualization as an image.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Captures the current Makerchip VIZ (Visual Debug) canvas visualization as an image data URL. The VIZ must already be rendered (code must be compiled first). Returns a base64-encoded data URL that can be used to display or analyze the visualization. Use this to capture and examine the current state of the VIZ visualization for debugging or documentation purposes.

quality.description: Image quality for jpeg/webp formats (0.0-1.0). Default is 1.0.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

saveToFile.description: Optional: save image to file for user visibility. If true, saves to /tmp with auto-generated name. If string, uses as file path. Image data is always returned to the model.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_layout_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get the current IDE pane layout configuration.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_set_layout_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Configure the IDE pane layout (create splits, arrange panes in tabs, set active pane).

state.description: Layout state defining pane arrangement (TabbedView or FlexSplit)

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

panes.description: Array of pane mnemonics in tab order

oneOf.description: Horizontal split with left and right sides
Vertical split with top and bottom sides

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_get_available_panes
languageModelTools.userDescription: List all available IDE panes with descriptions.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Retrieves a complete list of all panes in the Makerchip IDE, including main IDE panes, static panes (tutorials, documentation, etc.), and third-party panes (if any). Each pane entry includes its mnemonic (unique identifier used in layout operations), display name, description, and availability status.

MAIN IDE PANES (in VS Code extension context):
- Log: Compilation error log and messages
- Nav-TLV: TL-Verilog code navigation tree
- Diagram: Circuit diagram visualization
- Viz: VIZ canvas for visual debug
- Waveform: Waveform viewer for signals

NOTE: Editor pane is NOT available in VS Code extension (VS Code provides its own editor). The Editor will appear in the list with available=false.

STATIC PANES (always available, opened on demand):
- About, Help, Settings
- Tutorials (Makerchip Tutorial, Tutorial-Combo, Tutorial-Seq, Tutorial-Pipes, etc.)
- Documentation (VIZ Guide, TL-X Spec, M5 Spec, etc.)
- Courses and Videos
- Code Examples

THIRD-PARTY PANES (dynamically created):
- Custom panes added by the platform via openThirdPartyPane API
- Can include PDFs, iframes, or other external content
- Listed separately with their content type

Each pane has an 'available' field: true if the pane can be used, false if not available in this context (e.g., Editor is always false in VS Code extension).

Use this tool to: discover what panes are available for layout configuration, check which panes are actually usable in the current IDE instance, find the correct mnemonic for a specific pane, understand what content each pane provides, or present options to users. Only use panes with available=true in makerchip_set_layout_state calls.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_open_pane
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open or activate a specific IDE pane by mnemonic.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Opens and/or activates a pane by its mnemonic identifier.

This tool works with both:
- Static panes (tutorials, documentation, courses) - opened on demand
- Always-open main IDE panes (Log, Diagram, Waveform, etc.) can be activated (made visible)

Use makerchip_get_available_panes to discover valid pane mnemonics. Only use panes with available=true.

Common use cases:
- Open tutorials: "Makerchip Tutorial", "Combo Tutorial", "Pipelining Tutorial" (found in `resources/Makerchip-public/pane-blade`)
- Open documentation: "VIZ Guide", "TL-X Spec", "Help"
- Activate IDE panes: "Diagram", "Waveform", "Log"
- Open courses: "RISC-V Course+Single-Cycle"

ERROR HANDLING: Returns an error if the pane mnemonic is not found. The error message includes a complete list of available panes with their mnemonics and descriptions.

NOTE: For complex layout changes (splits, multiple panes), use makerchip_set_layout_state instead. This tool is best for simply opening/activating a single pane.

mnemonic.description: The mnemonic (unique identifier) of the pane to open or activate. Use makerchip_get_available_panes to discover valid mnemonics.

background.description: If true, opens the pane in the background without activating it. Default is false (activate the pane).

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_open_third_party_pane
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open custom content (PDFs, web pages) in IDE panes.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Opens a third-party pane in the Makerchip IDE to display external content like PDFs or web pages within the IDE's tabbed interface.

SUPPORTED CONTENT TYPES:

1. PDF (contentType: "pdf"):
   - Displays PDF files using the IDE's built-in PDF viewer
   - Supports any URL: https://, http://, file://, vscode-webview://, data URIs
   - Example: Technical documentation, datasheets, user guides

2. Iframe (contentType: "iframe"):
   - Embeds web pages in an iframe
   - Subject to browser same-origin policy and CSP restrictions
   - Target page must allow iframe embedding (no X-Frame-Options: DENY)
   - Example: External documentation sites, web tools, reference materials

PARAMETERS:

- mnemonic: Display name for the pane (appears in tab). Auto-uniquified if name conflicts occur.
- contentType: Either "pdf" or "iframe"
- contentParams.contentUrl: URL to the content (required)
- options.background: If true, opens without activating (default: false)
- options.nonTransferrable: If true, won't save pane data for layout recovery (default: false)

RETURN VALUE: The actual mnemonic (may differ if uniquified to avoid conflicts)

USE CASES:
- Display tutorial PDFs alongside code examples
- Show external API documentation while developing
- Embed reference materials or specification documents
- Open web-based tools or calculators within the IDE
- Present course materials or learning resources

EXAMPLES:

Open PDF tutorial:
{
  "mnemonic": "TL-Verilog Guide",
  "contentType": "pdf",
  "contentParams": {
    "contentUrl": "https://example.com/tlv-guide.pdf"
  }
}

Embed documentation website:
{
  "mnemonic": "API Docs",
  "contentType": "iframe",
  "contentParams": {
    "contentUrl": "https://docs.example.com"
  },
  "options": {
    "background": true
  }
}

NOTE: Third-party panes are tracked in layout state when forTransfer=true is used with makerchip_get_layout_state (unless nonTransferrable=true).

mnemonic.description: Display name for the pane (tab label). Will be uniquified automatically if a pane with this name already exists.

contentType.description: Type of content to display: 'pdf' for PDF viewer or 'iframe' for embedded web content.

contentUrl.description: URL to the content. For PDFs: supports https://, http://, file://, vscode-webview://, data URIs. For iframes: must support iframe embedding.

contentParams.description: Content parameters. Currently only contentUrl is supported, but future versions may add format-specific options.

background.description: If true, opens the pane without activating it (leaves current pane active). Default is false.

nonTransferrable.description: If true, pane construction data won't be stored for layout recovery. Use for temporary panes. Default is false.

panelName.description: Optional VS Code panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

chatParticipants.name: makerchip
chatParticipants.description: Makerchip IDE integration for TL-Verilog development

scripts.compile: tsc -p ./ && tsc -p src/tsconfig.webview.json && npm run copy-webview
scripts.watch: npm run copy-webview && tsc -watch -p ./ & tsc -watch -p src/tsconfig.webview.json & wait
scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
